summary: VitePress documentation site with 6 reference docs, landing page, custom theme, and GitHub Pages deployment workflow. Build succeeds. Two issues found: (1) deploy-docs.yaml uses unpinned tag-only action references (e.g., actions/checkout@v4) instead of SHA-pinned references (e.g., actions/checkout@<sha> # v6) used by all other workflows in the repo, and checkout@v4 is outdated vs v6 used elsewhere; (2) the deploy job environment URL references a nonexistent deployment payload field instead of the deploy-pages step output.

tests_checklist.item: npm run docs:build succeeds without errors
All 6 reference docs render as HTML in dist/reference/
Landing page renders with hero section

code_quality_checklist.item: package.json matches spec: name, scripts, devDependency
config.ts has correct title, base, sidebar, socialLinks
Theme and CSS match architecture/docs/.vitepress/theme/ pattern
Vue compiler options handle GitHub Actions ${{ }} syntax in reference docs
docs/index.md has hero, features, architecture docs link
Workflow uses .yaml extension and self-references correctly in paths
Action references use SHA-pinned format matching other workflows

security_checklist.item: Workflow permissions follow least privilege (contents:read, pages:write, id-token:write)
No user inputs interpolated in shell commands
No secrets exposed in workflow or config files
Action references pinned by SHA to prevent supply chain attacks

architecture_checklist.item: Two-job workflow structure (build -> deploy) matches spec
Concurrency group 'pages' with cancel-in-progress: false is correct for GitHub Pages
SPDX headers on all new files follow correct format per file type

dry_yagni_checklist.item: No duplicated logic beyond intentional CSS reuse from architecture site

fail_fast_checklist.item: npm ci used instead of npm install for deterministic builds

defensive_checklist.item: fetch-depth: 0 ensures VitePress git-based features work
Workflow triggers correctly scoped to docs-related paths

security_findings.description: PC3: All 5 existing workflow files use SHA-pinned action references (e.g., actions/checkout@de0fac2e4500dabe0009e67214ff5f5447ce83dd # v6). The deploy-docs.yaml uses tag-only references (e.g., actions/checkout@v4). Tag references are mutable and can be retargeted after a compromise of the upstream action repository. This is both a pattern inconsistency and a supply chain security concern.
security_findings.fix: Pin all action references by SHA with version comment. Use checkout v6 to match repo standard. Example: uses: actions/checkout@de0fac2e4500dabe0009e67214ff5f5447ce83dd # v6. Look up current SHAs for setup-node@v4, configure-pages@v5, upload-pages-artifact@v3, deploy-pages@v4.

architecture_findings.description: Q4: Deploy job environment URL uses ${{ github.event.deployment.payload.web_url || '' }} which references the GitHub deployment event payload. The deploy-pages@v4 action does not populate this field — it outputs the URL via step outputs. The environment URL in GitHub will always be empty.
architecture_findings.fix: Change url: ${{ github.event.deployment.payload.web_url || '' }} to url: ${{ steps.deployment.outputs.page_url }}

issues_found.description: Action references use mutable tags (e.g., @v4) instead of SHA-pinned references used by all other workflows. Additionally, actions/checkout@v4 is outdated — the repo uses v6.
issues_found.fix: Pin all 5 action references by SHA with version comments. Use v6 for checkout to match ci.yaml, build-images.yaml, etc. Look up current SHAs for: actions/checkout (v6), actions/setup-node (v4), actions/configure-pages (v5), actions/upload-pages-artifact (v3), actions/deploy-pages (v4).

issues_found.description: Environment URL references github.event.deployment.payload.web_url which is not populated by deploy-pages@v4. The GitHub environment page will show an empty URL.

next_steps: Pin all action references in deploy-docs.yaml by SHA with version comments, matching the pattern in ci.yaml and build-images.yaml
Fix environment URL to use steps.deployment.outputs.page_url
Update actions/checkout from v4 to v6 to match the rest of the repo

detected_patterns.name: SHA-pinned GitHub Actions references with version comment
detected_patterns.applies_when: Adding or updating a GitHub Actions workflow that references external actions
detected_patterns.description: All action references use the full SHA hash with a trailing version comment (e.g., actions/checkout@de0fac2e4500dabe0009e67214ff5f5447ce83dd # v6). This prevents supply chain attacks via mutable tag retargeting and provides audit traceability. The version comment preserves human readability. This extends the existing github-actions-workflow-spdx-header-and-structure-convention pattern.
